title: paraprasophyllum basalticum
side_effects: Paraprasophyllum basalticum can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and digestive issues. Ingestion of the plant can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, while topical application may cause redness, itching, and swelling. Prolonged exposure to the plant's extracts can also lead to kidney or liver damage in severe cases. Additionally, using Paraprasophyllum basalticum as a treatment for medical conditions without proper guidance can worsen underlying health issues or interact with other medications.
